76
77static user_id_t g_value_obj_uid = 0;
78
79// FIXME: this will return true for vector types whose elements
80// are floats. Audit all usages of this function and call
81// IsFloatingPointType() instead if vectors of floats aren't intended
82// to be supported.
83static bool HasFloatingRepresentation(CompilerType ct) {
84 return ct.GetTypeInfo() & eTypeIsFloat;
85}
86
87// ValueObject constructor
88ValueObject::ValueObject(ValueObject &parent)
89 : m_parent(&parent), m_update_point(parent.GetUpdatePoint()),
90 m_manager(parent.GetManager()), m_id(++g_value_obj_uid) {
91 m_flags.m_is_synthetic_children_generated =
92 parent.m_flags.m_is_synthetic_children_generated;
93 m_data.SetByteOrder(parent.GetDataExtractor().GetByteOrder());
94 m_data.SetAddressByteSize(parent.GetDataExtractor().GetAddressByteSize());
95 m_manager->ManageObject(this);
96}
97
98// ValueObject constructor
99ValueObject::ValueObject(ExecutionContextScope *exe_scope,
100 ValueObjectManager &manager,
101 AddressType child_ptr_or_ref_addr_type)
102 : m_update_point(exe_scope), m_manager(&manager),
103 m_address_type_of_ptr_or_ref_children(child_ptr_or_ref_addr_type),
104 m_id(++g_value_obj_uid) {
105 if (exe_scope) {
106 TargetSP target_sp(exe_scope->CalculateTarget());
107 if (target_sp) {
108 const ArchSpec &arch = target_sp->GetArchitecture();
109 m_data.SetByteOrder(arch.GetByteOrder());
110 m_data.SetAddressByteSize(arch.GetAddressByteSize());
111 }
112 }
113 m_manager->ManageObject(this);
114}
115
116// Destructor
117ValueObject::~ValueObject() = default;
118
119bool ValueObject::UpdateValueIfNeeded(bool update_format) {
120
121 bool did_change_formats = false;
122
123 if (update_format)
124 did_change_formats = UpdateFormatsIfNeeded();
125
126 // If this is a constant value, then our success is predicated on whether we
127 // have an error or not
128 if (GetIsConstant()) {
129 // if you are constant, things might still have changed behind your back
130 // (e.g. you are a frozen object and things have changed deeper than you
131 // cared to freeze-dry yourself) in this case, your value has not changed,
132 // but "computed" entries might have, so you might now have a different
133 // summary, or a different object description. clear these so we will
134 // recompute them
135 if (update_format && !did_change_formats)
136 ClearUserVisibleData(eClearUserVisibleDataItemsSummary |
137 eClearUserVisibleDataItemsDescription);
138 return m_error.Success();
139 }
140
141 bool first_update = IsChecksumEmpty();
142
143 if (NeedsUpdating()) {
144 m_update_point.SetUpdated();
145
146 // Save the old value using swap to avoid a string copy which also will
147 // clear our m_value_str
148 if (m_value_str.empty()) {
149 m_flags.m_old_value_valid = false;
150 } else {
151 m_flags.m_old_value_valid = true;
152 m_old_value_str.swap(m_value_str);
153 ClearUserVisibleData(eClearUserVisibleDataItemsValue);
154 }
155
156 ClearUserVisibleData();
157
158 if (IsInScope()) {
159 const bool value_was_valid = GetValueIsValid();
160 SetValueDidChange(false);
161
162 m_error.Clear();
163
164 // Call the pure virtual function to update the value
165
166 bool need_compare_checksums = false;
167 llvm::SmallVector<uint8_t, 16> old_checksum;
168
169 if (!first_update && CanProvideValue()) {
170 need_compare_checksums = true;
171 old_checksum.resize(m_value_checksum.size());
172 std::copy(m_value_checksum.begin(), m_value_checksum.end(),
173 old_checksum.begin());
174 }
175
176 bool success = UpdateValue();
177
178 SetValueIsValid(success);
179
180 if (success) {
181 UpdateChildrenAddressType();
182 const uint64_t max_checksum_size = 128;
183 m_data.Checksum(m_value_checksum, max_checksum_size);
184 } else {
185 need_compare_checksums = false;
186 m_value_checksum.clear();
187 }
188
189 assert(!need_compare_checksums ||
190 (!old_checksum.empty() && !m_value_checksum.empty()));
191
192 if (first_update)
193 SetValueDidChange(false);
194 else if (!m_flags.m_value_did_change && !success) {
195 // The value wasn't gotten successfully, so we mark this as changed if
196 // the value used to be valid and now isn't
197 SetValueDidChange(value_was_valid);
198 } else if (need_compare_checksums) {
199 SetValueDidChange(memcmp(&old_checksum[0], &m_value_checksum[0],
200 m_value_checksum.size()));
201 }
202
203 } else {
204 m_error = Status::FromErrorString("out of scope");
205 }
206 }
207 return m_error.Success();
208}
209
210bool ValueObject::UpdateFormatsIfNeeded() {
211 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::DataFormatters);
212 LLDB_LOGF(log,
213 "[%s %p] checking for FormatManager revisions. ValueObject "
214 "rev: %d - Global rev: %d",
215 GetName().GetCString(), static_cast<void *>(this),
216 m_last_format_mgr_revision,
217 DataVisualization::GetCurrentRevision());
218
219 bool any_change = false;
220
221 if ((m_last_format_mgr_revision != DataVisualization::GetCurrentRevision())) {
222 m_last_format_mgr_revision = DataVisualization::GetCurrentRevision();
223 any_change = true;
224
225 SetValueFormat(DataVisualization::GetFormat(*this, GetDynamicValueType()));
226 SetSummaryFormat(
227 DataVisualization::GetSummaryFormat(*this, GetDynamicValueType()));
228 SetSyntheticChildren(
229 DataVisualization::GetSyntheticChildren(*this, GetDynamicValueType()));
230 }
231
232 return any_change;
233}
234
235void ValueObject::SetNeedsUpdate() {
236 m_update_point.SetNeedsUpdate();
237 // We have to clear the value string here so ConstResult children will notice
238 // if their values are changed by hand (i.e. with SetValueAsCString).
239 ClearUserVisibleData(eClearUserVisibleDataItemsValue);
240}
241
242void ValueObject::ClearDynamicTypeInformation() {
243 m_flags.m_children_count_valid = false;
244 m_flags.m_did_calculate_complete_objc_class_type = false;
245 m_last_format_mgr_revision = 0;
246 m_override_type = CompilerType();
247 SetValueFormat(lldb::TypeFormatImplSP());
248 SetSummaryFormat(lldb::TypeSummaryImplSP());
249 SetSyntheticChildren(lldb::SyntheticChildrenSP());
250}
251
252CompilerType ValueObject::GetCompilerType() {
253 CompilerType compiler_type(GetCompilerTypeImpl());
254
255 if (m_flags.m_did_calculate_complete_objc_class_type) {
256 if (m_override_type.IsValid())
257 return m_override_type;
258 else
259 return compiler_type;
260 }
261
262 m_flags.m_did_calculate_complete_objc_class_type = true;
263
264 ProcessSP process_sp(
265 GetUpdatePoint().GetExecutionContextRef().GetProcessSP());
266
267 if (!process_sp)
268 return compiler_type;
269
270 if (auto *runtime =
271 process_sp->GetLanguageRuntime(GetObjectRuntimeLanguage())) {
272 if (std::optional<CompilerType> complete_type =
273 runtime->GetRuntimeType(compiler_type)) {
274 m_override_type = *complete_type;
275 if (m_override_type.IsValid())
276 return m_override_type;
277 }
278 }
279 return compiler_type;
280}
281
282DataExtractor &ValueObject::GetDataExtractor() {
283 UpdateValueIfNeeded(false);
284 return m_data;
285}
286
287const Status &ValueObject::GetError() {
288 UpdateValueIfNeeded(false);
289 return m_error;
290}
291
292const char *ValueObject::GetLocationAsCStringImpl(const Value &value,
293 const DataExtractor &data) {
294 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(false)) {
295 if (m_location_str.empty()) {
296 StreamString sstr;
297
298 Value::ValueType value_type = value.GetValueType();
299
300 switch (value_type) {
301 case Value::ValueType::Invalid:
302 m_location_str = "invalid";
303 break;
304 case Value::ValueType::Scalar:
305 if (value.GetContextType() == Value::ContextType::RegisterInfo) {
306 RegisterInfo *reg_info = value.GetRegisterInfo();
307 if (reg_info) {
308 if (reg_info->name)
309 m_location_str = reg_info->name;
310 else if (reg_info->alt_name)
311 m_location_str = reg_info->alt_name;
312 if (m_location_str.empty())
313 m_location_str = (reg_info->encoding == lldb::eEncodingVector)
314 ? "vector"
315 : "scalar";
316 }
317 }
318 if (m_location_str.empty())
319 m_location_str = "scalar";
320 break;
321
322 case Value::ValueType::LoadAddress:
323 case Value::ValueType::FileAddress:
324 case Value::ValueType::HostAddress: {
325 uint32_t addr_nibble_size = data.GetAddressByteSize() * 2;
326 sstr.Printf("0x%*.*llx", addr_nibble_size, addr_nibble_size,
327 value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS));
328 m_location_str = std::string(sstr.GetString());
329 } break;
330 }
331 }
332 }
333 return m_location_str.c_str();
334}
335
336bool ValueObject::ResolveValue(Scalar &scalar) {
337 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(
338 false)) // make sure that you are up to date before returning anything
339 {
340 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
341 Value tmp_value(m_value);
342 scalar = tmp_value.ResolveValue(&exe_ctx, GetModule().get());
343 if (scalar.IsValid()) {
344 const uint32_t bitfield_bit_size = GetBitfieldBitSize();
345 if (bitfield_bit_size)
346 return scalar.ExtractBitfield(bitfield_bit_size,
347 GetBitfieldBitOffset());
348 return true;
349 }
350 }
351 return false;
352}
353
354bool ValueObject::IsLogicalTrue(Status &error) {
355 if (Language *language = Language::FindPlugin(GetObjectRuntimeLanguage())) {
356 LazyBool is_logical_true = language->IsLogicalTrue(*this, error);
357 switch (is_logical_true) {
358 case eLazyBoolYes:
359 case eLazyBoolNo:
360 return (is_logical_true == true);
361 case eLazyBoolCalculate:
362 break;
363 }
364 }
365
366 Scalar scalar_value;
367
368 if (!ResolveValue(scalar_value)) {
369 error = Status::FromErrorString("failed to get a scalar result");
370 return false;
371 }
372
373 bool ret;
374 ret = scalar_value.ULongLong(1) != 0;
375 error.Clear();
376 return ret;
377}
378
379ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetChildAtIndex(uint32_t idx, bool can_create) {
380 ValueObjectSP child_sp;
381 // We may need to update our value if we are dynamic
382 if (IsPossibleDynamicType())
383 UpdateValueIfNeeded(false);
384 if (idx < GetNumChildrenIgnoringErrors()) {
385 // Check if we have already made the child value object?
386 if (can_create && !m_children.HasChildAtIndex(idx)) {
387 // No we haven't created the child at this index, so lets have our
388 // subclass do it and cache the result for quick future access.
389 m_children.SetChildAtIndex(idx, CreateChildAtIndex(idx));
390 }
391
392 ValueObject *child = m_children.GetChildAtIndex(idx);
393 if (child != nullptr)
394 return child->GetSP();
395 }
396 return child_sp;
397}
398
399lldb::ValueObjectSP
400ValueObject::GetChildAtNamePath(llvm::ArrayRef<llvm::StringRef> names) {
401 if (names.size() == 0)
402 return GetSP();
403 ValueObjectSP root(GetSP());
404 for (llvm::StringRef name : names) {
405 root = root->GetChildMemberWithName(name);
406 if (!root) {
407 return root;
408 }
409 }
410 return root;
411}
412
413llvm::Expected<size_t>
414ValueObject::GetIndexOfChildWithName(llvm::StringRef name) {
415 bool omit_empty_base_classes = true;
416 return GetCompilerType().GetIndexOfChildWithName(name,
417 omit_empty_base_classes);
418}
419
420ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetChildMemberWithName(llvm::StringRef name,
421 bool can_create) {
422 // We may need to update our value if we are dynamic.
423 if (IsPossibleDynamicType())
424 UpdateValueIfNeeded(false);
425
426 // When getting a child by name, it could be buried inside some base classes
427 // (which really aren't part of the expression path), so we need a vector of
428 // indexes that can get us down to the correct child.
429 std::vector<uint32_t> child_indexes;
430 bool omit_empty_base_classes = true;
431
432 if (!GetCompilerType().IsValid())
433 return ValueObjectSP();
434
435 const size_t num_child_indexes =
436 GetCompilerType().GetIndexOfChildMemberWithName(
437 name, omit_empty_base_classes, child_indexes);
438 if (num_child_indexes == 0)
439 return nullptr;
440
441 ValueObjectSP child_sp = GetSP();
442 for (uint32_t idx : child_indexes)
443 if (child_sp)
444 child_sp = child_sp->GetChildAtIndex(idx, can_create);
445 return child_sp;
446}
447
448llvm::Expected<uint32_t> ValueObject::GetNumChildren(uint32_t max) {
449 UpdateValueIfNeeded();
450
451 if (max < UINT32_MAX) {
452 if (m_flags.m_children_count_valid) {
453 size_t children_count = m_children.GetChildrenCount();
454 return children_count <= max ? children_count : max;
455 } else
456 return CalculateNumChildren(max);
457 }
458
459 if (!m_flags.m_children_count_valid) {
460 auto num_children_or_err = CalculateNumChildren();
461 if (num_children_or_err)
462 SetNumChildren(*num_children_or_err);
463 else
464 return num_children_or_err;
465 }
466 return m_children.GetChildrenCount();
467}
468
469uint32_t ValueObject::GetNumChildrenIgnoringErrors(uint32_t max) {
470 auto value_or_err = GetNumChildren(max);
471 if (value_or_err)
472 return *value_or_err;
473 LLDB_LOG_ERRORV(GetLog(LLDBLog::DataFormatters), value_or_err.takeError(),
474 "{0}");
475 return 0;
476}
477
478bool ValueObject::MightHaveChildren() {
479 bool has_children = false;
480 const uint32_t type_info = GetTypeInfo();
481 if (type_info) {
482 if (type_info & (eTypeHasChildren | eTypeIsPointer | eTypeIsReference))
483 has_children = true;
484 } else {
485 has_children = GetNumChildrenIgnoringErrors() > 0;
486 }
487 return has_children;
488}
489
490// Should only be called by ValueObject::GetNumChildren()
491void ValueObject::SetNumChildren(uint32_t num_children) {
492 m_flags.m_children_count_valid = true;
493 m_children.SetChildrenCount(num_children);
494}
495
496ValueObject *ValueObject::CreateChildAtIndex(size_t idx) {
497 bool omit_empty_base_classes = true;
498 bool ignore_array_bounds = false;
499 std::string child_name;
500 uint32_t child_byte_size = 0;
501 int32_t child_byte_offset = 0;
502 uint32_t child_bitfield_bit_size = 0;
503 uint32_t child_bitfield_bit_offset = 0;
504 bool child_is_base_class = false;
505 bool child_is_deref_of_parent = false;
506 uint64_t language_flags = 0;
507 const bool transparent_pointers = true;
508
509 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
510
511 auto child_compiler_type_or_err =
512 GetCompilerType().GetChildCompilerTypeAtIndex(
513 &exe_ctx, idx, transparent_pointers, omit_empty_base_classes,
514 ignore_array_bounds, child_name, child_byte_size, child_byte_offset,
515 child_bitfield_bit_size, child_bitfield_bit_offset,
516 child_is_base_class, child_is_deref_of_parent, this, language_flags);
517 if (!child_compiler_type_or_err || !child_compiler_type_or_err->IsValid()) {
518 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Types),
519 child_compiler_type_or_err.takeError(),
520 "could not find child: {0}");
521 return nullptr;
522 }
523
524 return new ValueObjectChild(
525 *this, *child_compiler_type_or_err, ConstString(child_name),
526 child_byte_size, child_byte_offset, child_bitfield_bit_size,
527 child_bitfield_bit_offset, child_is_base_class, child_is_deref_of_parent,
528 eAddressTypeInvalid, language_flags);
529}
530
531ValueObject *ValueObject::CreateSyntheticArrayMember(size_t idx) {
532 bool omit_empty_base_classes = true;
533 bool ignore_array_bounds = true;
534 std::string child_name;
535 uint32_t child_byte_size = 0;
536 int32_t child_byte_offset = 0;
537 uint32_t child_bitfield_bit_size = 0;
538 uint32_t child_bitfield_bit_offset = 0;
539 bool child_is_base_class = false;
540 bool child_is_deref_of_parent = false;
541 uint64_t language_flags = 0;
542 const bool transparent_pointers = false;
543
544 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
545
546 auto child_compiler_type_or_err =
547 GetCompilerType().GetChildCompilerTypeAtIndex(
548 &exe_ctx, 0, transparent_pointers, omit_empty_base_classes,
549 ignore_array_bounds, child_name, child_byte_size, child_byte_offset,
550 child_bitfield_bit_size, child_bitfield_bit_offset,
551 child_is_base_class, child_is_deref_of_parent, this, language_flags);
552 if (!child_compiler_type_or_err) {
553 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Types),
554 child_compiler_type_or_err.takeError(),
555 "could not find child: {0}");
556 return nullptr;
557 }
558
559 if (child_compiler_type_or_err->IsValid()) {
560 child_byte_offset += child_byte_size * idx;
561
562 return new ValueObjectChild(
563 *this, *child_compiler_type_or_err, ConstString(child_name),
564 child_byte_size, child_byte_offset, child_bitfield_bit_size,
565 child_bitfield_bit_offset, child_is_base_class,
566 child_is_deref_of_parent, eAddressTypeInvalid, language_flags);
567 }
568
569 // In case of an incomplete type, try to use the ValueObject's
570 // synthetic value to create the child ValueObject.
571 if (ValueObjectSP synth_valobj_sp = GetSyntheticValue())
572 return synth_valobj_sp->GetChildAtIndex(idx, /*can_create=*/true).get();
573
574 return nullptr;
575}
576
577bool ValueObject::GetSummaryAsCString(TypeSummaryImpl *summary_ptr,
578 std::string &destination,
579 lldb::LanguageType lang) {
580 return GetSummaryAsCString(summary_ptr, destination,
581 TypeSummaryOptions().SetLanguage(lang));
582}
583
584bool ValueObject::GetSummaryAsCString(TypeSummaryImpl *summary_ptr,
585 std::string &destination,
586 const TypeSummaryOptions &options) {
587 destination.clear();
588
589 // If we have a forcefully completed type, don't try and show a summary from
590 // a valid summary string or function because the type is not complete and
591 // no member variables or member functions will be available.
592 if (GetCompilerType().IsForcefullyCompleted()) {
593 destination = "<incomplete type>";
594 return true;
595 }
596
597 // ideally we would like to bail out if passing NULL, but if we do so we end
598 // up not providing the summary for function pointers anymore
599 if (/*summary_ptr == NULL ||*/ m_flags.m_is_getting_summary)
600 return false;
601
602 m_flags.m_is_getting_summary = true;
603
604 TypeSummaryOptions actual_options(options);
605
606 if (actual_options.GetLanguage() == lldb::eLanguageTypeUnknown)
607 actual_options.SetLanguage(GetPreferredDisplayLanguage());
608
609 // this is a hot path in code and we prefer to avoid setting this string all
610 // too often also clearing out other information that we might care to see in
611 // a crash log. might be useful in very specific situations though.
612 /*Host::SetCrashDescriptionWithFormat("Trying to fetch a summary for %s %s.
613 Summary provider's description is %s",
614 GetTypeName().GetCString(),
615 GetName().GetCString(),
616 summary_ptr->GetDescription().c_str());*/
617
618 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(false) && summary_ptr) {
619 if (HasSyntheticValue())
620 m_synthetic_value->UpdateValueIfNeeded(); // the summary might depend on
621 // the synthetic children being
622 // up-to-date (e.g. ${svar%#})
623
624 if (TargetSP target_sp = GetExecutionContextRef().GetTargetSP()) {
625 SummaryStatisticsSP stats_sp =
626 target_sp->GetSummaryStatisticsCache()
627 .GetSummaryStatisticsForProvider(*summary_ptr);
628
629 // Construct RAII types to time and collect data on summary creation.
630 SummaryStatistics::SummaryInvocation invocation(stats_sp);
631 summary_ptr->FormatObject(this, destination, actual_options);
632 } else
633 summary_ptr->FormatObject(this, destination, actual_options);
634 }
635 m_flags.m_is_getting_summary = false;
636 return !destination.empty();
637}
638
639const char *ValueObject::GetSummaryAsCString(lldb::LanguageType lang) {
640 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(true) && m_summary_str.empty()) {
641 TypeSummaryOptions summary_options;
642 summary_options.SetLanguage(lang);
643 GetSummaryAsCString(GetSummaryFormat().get(), m_summary_str,
644 summary_options);
645 }
646 if (m_summary_str.empty())
647 return nullptr;
648 return m_summary_str.c_str();
649}
650
651bool ValueObject::GetSummaryAsCString(std::string &destination,
652 const TypeSummaryOptions &options) {
653 return GetSummaryAsCString(GetSummaryFormat().get(), destination, options);
654}
655
656bool ValueObject::IsCStringContainer(bool check_pointer) {
657 CompilerType pointee_or_element_compiler_type;
658 const Flags type_flags(GetTypeInfo(&pointee_or_element_compiler_type));
659 bool is_char_arr_ptr(type_flags.AnySet(eTypeIsArray | eTypeIsPointer) &&
660 pointee_or_element_compiler_type.IsCharType());
661 if (!is_char_arr_ptr)
662 return false;
663 if (!check_pointer)
664 return true;
665 if (type_flags.Test(eTypeIsArray))
666 return true;
667 addr_t cstr_address = GetPointerValue().address;
668 return (cstr_address != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
669}
670
671size_t ValueObject::GetPointeeData(DataExtractor &data, uint32_t item_idx,
672 uint32_t item_count) {
673 CompilerType pointee_or_element_compiler_type;
674 const uint32_t type_info = GetTypeInfo(&pointee_or_element_compiler_type);
675 const bool is_pointer_type = type_info & eTypeIsPointer;
676 const bool is_array_type = type_info & eTypeIsArray;
677 if (!(is_pointer_type || is_array_type))
678 return 0;
679
680 if (item_count == 0)
681 return 0;
682
683 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
684
685 std::optional<uint64_t> item_type_size =
686 llvm::expectedToOptional(pointee_or_element_compiler_type.GetByteSize(
687 exe_ctx.GetBestExecutionContextScope()));
688 if (!item_type_size)
689 return 0;
690 const uint64_t bytes = item_count * *item_type_size;
691 const uint64_t offset = item_idx * *item_type_size;
692
693 if (item_idx == 0 && item_count == 1) // simply a deref
694 {
695 if (is_pointer_type) {
696 Status error;
697 ValueObjectSP pointee_sp = Dereference(error);
698 if (error.Fail() || pointee_sp.get() == nullptr)
699 return 0;
700 return pointee_sp->GetData(data, error);
701 } else {
702 ValueObjectSP child_sp = GetChildAtIndex(0);
703 if (child_sp.get() == nullptr)
704 return 0;
705 Status error;
706 return child_sp->GetData(data, error);
707 }
708 return true;
709 } else /* (items > 1) */
710 {
711 Status error;
712 lldb_private::DataBufferHeap *heap_buf_ptr = nullptr;
713 lldb::DataBufferSP data_sp(heap_buf_ptr =
714 new lldb_private::DataBufferHeap());
715
716 auto [addr, addr_type] =
717 is_pointer_type ? GetPointerValue() : GetAddressOf(true);
718
719 switch (addr_type) {
720 case eAddressTypeFile: {
721 ModuleSP module_sp(GetModule());
722 if (module_sp) {
723 addr = addr + offset;
724 Address so_addr;
725 module_sp->ResolveFileAddress(addr, so_addr);
726 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
727 Target *target = exe_ctx.GetTargetPtr();
728 if (target) {
729 heap_buf_ptr->SetByteSize(bytes);
730 size_t bytes_read = target->ReadMemory(
731 so_addr, heap_buf_ptr->GetBytes(), bytes, error, true);
732 if (error.Success()) {
733 data.SetData(data_sp);
734 return bytes_read;
735 }
736 }
737 }
738 } break;
739 case eAddressTypeLoad: {
740 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
741 if (Target *target = exe_ctx.GetTargetPtr()) {
742 heap_buf_ptr->SetByteSize(bytes);
743 Address target_addr;
744 target_addr.SetLoadAddress(addr + offset, target);
745 size_t bytes_read =
746 target->ReadMemory(target_addr, heap_buf_ptr->GetBytes(), bytes,
747 error, /*force_live_memory=*/true);
748 if (error.Success() || bytes_read > 0) {
749 data.SetData(data_sp);
750 return bytes_read;
751 }
752 }
753 } break;
754 case eAddressTypeHost: {
755 auto max_bytes = llvm::expectedToOptional(GetCompilerType().GetByteSize(
756 exe_ctx.GetBestExecutionContextScope()));
757 if (max_bytes && *max_bytes > offset) {
758 size_t bytes_read = std::min<uint64_t>(*max_bytes - offset, bytes);
759 addr = m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
760 if (addr == 0 || addr == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
761 break;
762 heap_buf_ptr->CopyData((uint8_t *)(addr + offset), bytes_read);
763 data.SetData(data_sp);
764 return bytes_read;
765 }
766 } break;
767 case eAddressTypeInvalid:
768 break;
769 }
770 }
771 return 0;
772}
773
774uint64_t ValueObject::GetData(DataExtractor &data, Status &error) {
775 UpdateValueIfNeeded(false);
776 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
777 error = m_value.GetValueAsData(&exe_ctx, data, GetModule().get());
778 if (error.Fail()) {
779 if (m_data.GetByteSize()) {
780 data = m_data;
781 error.Clear();
782 return data.GetByteSize();
783 } else {
784 return 0;
785 }
786 }
787 data.SetAddressByteSize(m_data.GetAddressByteSize());
788 data.SetByteOrder(m_data.GetByteOrder());
789 return data.GetByteSize();
790}
791
792bool ValueObject::SetData(DataExtractor &data, Status &error) {
793 error.Clear();
794 // Make sure our value is up to date first so that our location and location
795 // type is valid.
796 if (!UpdateValueIfNeeded(false)) {
797 error = Status::FromErrorString("unable to read value");
798 return false;
799 }
800
801 const Encoding encoding = GetCompilerType().GetEncoding();
802
803 const size_t byte_size = llvm::expectedToOptional(GetByteSize()).value_or(0);
804
805 Value::ValueType value_type = m_value.GetValueType();
806
807 switch (value_type) {
808 case Value::ValueType::Invalid:
809 error = Status::FromErrorString("invalid location");
810 return false;
811 case Value::ValueType::Scalar: {
812 Status set_error =
813 m_value.GetScalar().SetValueFromData(data, encoding, byte_size);
814
815 if (!set_error.Success()) {
816 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
817 "unable to set scalar value: %s", set_error.AsCString());
818 return false;
819 }
820 } break;
821 case Value::ValueType::LoadAddress: {
822 // If it is a load address, then the scalar value is the storage location
823 // of the data, and we have to shove this value down to that load location.
824 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
825 Process *process = exe_ctx.GetProcessPtr();
826 if (process) {
827 addr_t target_addr = m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
828 size_t bytes_written = process->WriteMemory(
829 target_addr, data.GetDataStart(), byte_size, error);
830 if (!error.Success())
831 return false;
832 if (bytes_written != byte_size) {
833 error = Status::FromErrorString("unable to write value to memory");
834 return false;
835 }
836 }
837 } break;
838 case Value::ValueType::HostAddress: {
839 // If it is a host address, then we stuff the scalar as a DataBuffer into
840 // the Value's data.
841 DataBufferSP buffer_sp(new DataBufferHeap(byte_size, 0));
842 m_data.SetData(buffer_sp, 0);
843 data.CopyByteOrderedData(0, byte_size,
844 const_cast<uint8_t *>(m_data.GetDataStart()),
845 byte_size, m_data.GetByteOrder());
846 m_value.GetScalar() = (uintptr_t)m_data.GetDataStart();
847 } break;
848 case Value::ValueType::FileAddress:
849 break;
850 }
851
852 // If we have reached this point, then we have successfully changed the
853 // value.
854 SetNeedsUpdate();
855 return true;
856}
857
858llvm::ArrayRef<uint8_t> ValueObject::GetLocalBuffer() const {
859 if (m_value.GetValueType() != Value::ValueType::HostAddress)
860 return {};
861 auto start = m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
862 if (start == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
863 return {};
864 // Does our pointer point to this value object's m_data buffer?
865 if ((uint64_t)m_data.GetDataStart() == start)
866 return m_data.GetData();
867 // Does our pointer point to the value's buffer?
868 if ((uint64_t)m_value.GetBuffer().GetBytes() == start)
869 return m_value.GetBuffer().GetData();
870 // Our pointer points to something else. We can't know what the size is.
871 return {};
872}
873
874static bool CopyStringDataToBufferSP(const StreamString &source,
875 lldb::WritableDataBufferSP &destination) {
876 llvm::StringRef src = source.GetString();
877 src = src.rtrim('\0');
878 destination = std::make_shared<DataBufferHeap>(src.size(), 0);
879 memcpy(destination->GetBytes(), src.data(), src.size());
880 return true;
881}
882
883std::pair<size_t, bool>
884ValueObject::ReadPointedString(lldb::WritableDataBufferSP &buffer_sp,
885 Status &error, bool honor_array) {
886 bool was_capped = false;
887 StreamString s;
888 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
889 Target *target = exe_ctx.GetTargetPtr();
890
891 if (!target) {
892 s << "<no target to read from>";
893 error = Status::FromErrorString("no target to read from");
894 CopyStringDataToBufferSP(s, buffer_sp);
895 return {0, was_capped};
896 }
897
898 const auto max_length = target->GetMaximumSizeOfStringSummary();
899
900 size_t bytes_read = 0;
901 size_t total_bytes_read = 0;
902
903 CompilerType compiler_type = GetCompilerType();
904 CompilerType elem_or_pointee_compiler_type;
905 const Flags type_flags(GetTypeInfo(&elem_or_pointee_compiler_type));
906 if (type_flags.AnySet(eTypeIsArray | eTypeIsPointer) &&
907 elem_or_pointee_compiler_type.IsCharType()) {
908 AddrAndType cstr_address;
909
910 size_t cstr_len = 0;
911 bool capped_data = false;
912 const bool is_array = type_flags.Test(eTypeIsArray);
913 if (is_array) {
914 // We have an array
915 uint64_t array_size = 0;
916 if (compiler_type.IsArrayType(nullptr, &array_size)) {
917 cstr_len = array_size;
918 if (cstr_len > max_length) {
919 capped_data = true;
920 cstr_len = max_length;
921 }
922 }
923 cstr_address = GetAddressOf(true);
924 } else {
925 // We have a pointer
926 cstr_address = GetPointerValue();
927 }
928
929 if (cstr_address.address == 0 ||
930 cstr_address.address == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
931 if (cstr_address.type == eAddressTypeHost && is_array) {
932 const char *cstr = GetDataExtractor().PeekCStr(0);
933 if (cstr == nullptr) {
934 s << "<invalid address>";
935 error = Status::FromErrorString("invalid address");
936 CopyStringDataToBufferSP(s, buffer_sp);
937 return {0, was_capped};
938 }
939 s << llvm::StringRef(cstr, cstr_len);
940 CopyStringDataToBufferSP(s, buffer_sp);
941 return {cstr_len, was_capped};
942 } else {
943 s << "<invalid address>";
944 error = Status::FromErrorString("invalid address");
945 CopyStringDataToBufferSP(s, buffer_sp);
946 return {0, was_capped};
947 }
948 }
949
950 Address cstr_so_addr(cstr_address.address);
951 DataExtractor data;
952 if (cstr_len > 0 && honor_array) {
953 // I am using GetPointeeData() here to abstract the fact that some
954 // ValueObjects are actually frozen pointers in the host but the pointed-
955 // to data lives in the debuggee, and GetPointeeData() automatically
956 // takes care of this
957 GetPointeeData(data, 0, cstr_len);
958
959 if ((bytes_read = data.GetByteSize()) > 0) {
960 total_bytes_read = bytes_read;
961 for (size_t offset = 0; offset < bytes_read; offset++)
962 s.Printf("%c", *data.PeekData(offset, 1));
963 if (capped_data)
964 was_capped = true;
965 }
966 } else {
967 cstr_len = max_length;
968 const size_t k_max_buf_size = 64;
969
970 size_t offset = 0;
971
972 int cstr_len_displayed = -1;
973 bool capped_cstr = false;
974 // I am using GetPointeeData() here to abstract the fact that some
975 // ValueObjects are actually frozen pointers in the host but the pointed-
976 // to data lives in the debuggee, and GetPointeeData() automatically
977 // takes care of this
978 while ((bytes_read = GetPointeeData(data, offset, k_max_buf_size)) > 0) {
979 total_bytes_read += bytes_read;
980 const char *cstr = data.PeekCStr(0);
981 size_t len = strnlen(cstr, k_max_buf_size);
982 if (cstr_len_displayed < 0)
983 cstr_len_displayed = len;
984
985 if (len == 0)
986 break;
987 cstr_len_displayed += len;
988 if (len > bytes_read)
989 len = bytes_read;
990 if (len > cstr_len)
991 len = cstr_len;
992
993 for (size_t offset = 0; offset < bytes_read; offset++)
994 s.Printf("%c", *data.PeekData(offset, 1));
995
996 if (len < k_max_buf_size)
997 break;
998
999 if (len >= cstr_len) {
1000 capped_cstr = true;
1001 break;
1002 }
1003
1004 cstr_len -= len;
1005 offset += len;
1006 }
1007
1008 if (cstr_len_displayed >= 0) {
1009 if (capped_cstr)
1010 was_capped = true;
1011 }
1012 }
1013 } else {
1014 error = Status::FromErrorString("not a string object");
1015 s << "<not a string object>";
1016 }
1017 CopyStringDataToBufferSP(s, buffer_sp);
1018 return {total_bytes_read, was_capped};
1019}
1020
1021llvm::Expected<std::string> ValueObject::GetObjectDescription() {
1022 if (!UpdateValueIfNeeded(true))
1023 return llvm::createStringError("could not update value");
1024
1025 // Return cached value.
1026 if (!m_object_desc_str.empty())
1027 return m_object_desc_str;
1028
1029 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
1030 Process *process = exe_ctx.GetProcessPtr();
1031 if (!process)
1032 return llvm::createStringError("no process");
1033
1034 // Returns the object description produced by one language runtime.
1035 auto get_object_description =
1036 [&](LanguageType language) -> llvm::Expected<std::string> {
1037 if (LanguageRuntime *runtime = process->GetLanguageRuntime(language)) {
1038 StreamString s;
1039 if (llvm::Error error = runtime->GetObjectDescription(s, *this))
1040 return error;
1041 m_object_desc_str = s.GetString();
1042 return m_object_desc_str;
1043 }
1044 return llvm::createStringError("no native language runtime");
1045 };
1046
1047 // Try the native language runtime first.
1048 LanguageType native_language = GetObjectRuntimeLanguage();
1049 llvm::Expected<std::string> desc = get_object_description(native_language);
1050 if (desc)
1051 return desc;
1052
1053 // Try the Objective-C language runtime. This fallback is necessary
1054 // for Objective-C++ and mixed Objective-C / C++ programs.
1055 if (Language::LanguageIsCFamily(native_language)) {
1056 // We're going to try again, so let's drop the first error.
1057 llvm::consumeError(desc.takeError());
1058 return get_object_description(eLanguageTypeObjC);
1059 }
1060 return desc;
1061}
1062
1063bool ValueObject::GetValueAsCString(const lldb_private::TypeFormatImpl &format,
1064 std::string &destination) {
1065 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(false))
1066 return format.FormatObject(this, destination);
1067 else
1068 return false;
1069}
1070
1071bool ValueObject::GetValueAsCString(lldb::Format format,
1072 std::string &destination) {
1073 return GetValueAsCString(TypeFormatImpl_Format(format), destination);
1074}
1075
1076const char *ValueObject::GetValueAsCString() {
1077 if (UpdateValueIfNeeded(true)) {
1078 lldb::TypeFormatImplSP format_sp;
1079 lldb::Format my_format = GetFormat();
1080 if (my_format == lldb::eFormatDefault) {
1081 if (m_type_format_sp)
1082 format_sp = m_type_format_sp;
1083 else {
1084 if (m_flags.m_is_bitfield_for_scalar)
1085 my_format = eFormatUnsigned;
1086 else {
1087 if (m_value.GetContextType() == Value::ContextType::RegisterInfo) {
1088 const RegisterInfo *reg_info = m_value.GetRegisterInfo();
1089 if (reg_info)
1090 my_format = reg_info->format;
1091 } else {
1092 my_format = GetValue().GetCompilerType().GetFormat();
1093 }
1094 }
1095 }
1096 }
1097 if (my_format != m_last_format || m_value_str.empty()) {
1098 m_last_format = my_format;
1099 if (!format_sp)
1100 format_sp = std::make_shared<TypeFormatImpl_Format>(my_format);
1101 if (GetValueAsCString(*format_sp.get(), m_value_str)) {
1102 if (!m_flags.m_value_did_change && m_flags.m_old_value_valid) {
1103 // The value was gotten successfully, so we consider the value as
1104 // changed if the value string differs
1105 SetValueDidChange(m_old_value_str != m_value_str);
1106 }
1107 }
1108 }
1109 }
1110 if (m_value_str.empty())
1111 return nullptr;
1112 return m_value_str.c_str();
1113}
1114
1115// if > 8bytes, 0 is returned. this method should mostly be used to read
1116// address values out of pointers
1117uint64_t ValueObject::GetValueAsUnsigned(uint64_t fail_value, bool *success) {
1118 // If our byte size is zero this is an aggregate type that has children
1119 if (CanProvideValue()) {
1120 Scalar scalar;
1121 if (ResolveValue(scalar)) {
1122 if (success)
1123 *success = true;
1124 scalar.MakeUnsigned();
1125 return scalar.ULongLong(fail_value);
1126 }
1127 // fallthrough, otherwise...
1128 }
1129
1130 if (success)
1131 *success = false;
1132 return fail_value;
1133}
1134
1135int64_t ValueObject::GetValueAsSigned(int64_t fail_value, bool *success) {
1136 // If our byte size is zero this is an aggregate type that has children
1137 if (CanProvideValue()) {
1138 Scalar scalar;
1139 if (ResolveValue(scalar)) {
1140 if (success)
1141 *success = true;
1142 scalar.MakeSigned();
1143 return scalar.SLongLong(fail_value);
1144 }
1145 // fallthrough, otherwise...
1146 }
1147
1148 if (success)
1149 *success = false;
1150 return fail_value;
1151}
1152
1153llvm::Expected<llvm::APSInt> ValueObject::GetValueAsAPSInt() {
1154 // Make sure the type can be converted to an APSInt.
1155 if (!GetCompilerType().IsInteger() &&
1156 !GetCompilerType().IsScopedEnumerationType() &&
1157 !GetCompilerType().IsEnumerationType() &&
1158 !GetCompilerType().IsPointerType() &&
1159 !GetCompilerType().IsNullPtrType() &&
1160 !GetCompilerType().IsReferenceType() && !GetCompilerType().IsBoolean())
1161 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>(
1162 "type cannot be converted to APSInt", llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
1163
1164 if (CanProvideValue()) {
1165 Scalar scalar;
1166 if (ResolveValue(scalar))
1167 return scalar.GetAPSInt();
1168 }
1169
1170 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>(
1171 "error occurred; unable to convert to APSInt",
1172 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
1173}
1174
1175llvm::Expected<llvm::APFloat> ValueObject::GetValueAsAPFloat() {
1176 if (!HasFloatingRepresentation(GetCompilerType()))
1177 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>(
1178 "type cannot be converted to APFloat", llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
1179
1180 if (CanProvideValue()) {
1181 Scalar scalar;
1182 if (ResolveValue(scalar))
1183 return scalar.GetAPFloat();
1184 }
1185
1186 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>(
1187 "error occurred; unable to convert to APFloat",
1188 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
1189}
1190
1191llvm::Expected<bool> ValueObject::GetValueAsBool() {
1192 CompilerType val_type = GetCompilerType();
1193 if (val_type.IsInteger() || val_type.IsUnscopedEnumerationType() ||
1194 val_type.IsPointerType()) {
1195 auto value_or_err = GetValueAsAPSInt();
1196 if (value_or_err)
1197 return value_or_err->getBoolValue();
1198 }
1199 if (HasFloatingRepresentation(val_type)) {
1200 auto value_or_err = GetValueAsAPFloat();
1201 if (value_or_err)
1202 return value_or_err->isNonZero();
1203 }
1204 if (val_type.IsArrayType())
1205 return GetAddressOf().address != 0;
1206
1207 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>("type cannot be converted to bool",
1208 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
1209}
1210
1211void ValueObject::SetValueFromInteger(const llvm::APInt &value, Status &error) {
1212 // Verify the current object is an integer object
1213 CompilerType val_type = GetCompilerType();
1214 if (!val_type.IsInteger() && !val_type.IsUnscopedEnumerationType() &&
1215 !HasFloatingRepresentation(val_type) && !val_type.IsPointerType() &&
1216 !val_type.IsScalarType()) {
1217 error =
1218 Status::FromErrorString("current value object is not an integer objet");
1219 return;
1220 }
1221
1222 // Verify the current object is not actually associated with any program
1223 // variable.
1224 if (GetVariable()) {
1225 error = Status::FromErrorString(
1226 "current value object is not a temporary object");
1227 return;
1228 }
1229
1230 // Verify the proposed new value is the right size.
1231 lldb::TargetSP target = GetTargetSP();
1232 uint64_t byte_size = 0;
1233 if (auto temp =
1234 llvm::expectedToOptional(GetCompilerType().GetByteSize(target.get())))
1235 byte_size = temp.value();
1236 if (value.getBitWidth() != byte_size * CHAR_BIT) {
1237 error = Status::FromErrorString(
1238 "illegal argument: new value should be of the same size");
1239 return;
1240 }
1241
1242 lldb::DataExtractorSP data_sp;
1243 data_sp->SetData(value.getRawData(), byte_size,
1244 target->GetArchitecture().GetByteOrder());
1245 data_sp->SetAddressByteSize(
1246 static_cast<uint8_t>(target->GetArchitecture().GetAddressByteSize()));
1247 SetData(*data_sp, error);
1248}
1249
1250void ValueObject::SetValueFromInteger(lldb::ValueObjectSP new_val_sp,
1251 Status &error) {
1252 // Verify the current object is an integer object
1253 CompilerType val_type = GetCompilerType();
1254 if (!val_type.IsInteger() && !val_type.IsUnscopedEnumerationType() &&
1255 !HasFloatingRepresentation(val_type) && !val_type.IsPointerType() &&
1256 !val_type.IsScalarType()) {
1257 error =
1258 Status::FromErrorString("current value object is not an integer objet");
1259 return;
1260 }
1261
1262 // Verify the current object is not actually associated with any program
1263 // variable.
1264 if (GetVariable()) {
1265 error = Status::FromErrorString(
1266 "current value object is not a temporary object");
1267 return;
1268 }
1269
1270 // Verify the proposed new value is the right type.
1271 CompilerType new_val_type = new_val_sp->GetCompilerType();
1272 if (!new_val_type.IsInteger() && !HasFloatingRepresentation(new_val_type) &&
1273 !new_val_type.IsPointerType()) {
1274 error = Status::FromErrorString(
1275 "illegal argument: new value should be of the same size");
1276 return;
1277 }
1278
1279 if (new_val_type.IsInteger()) {
1280 auto value_or_err = new_val_sp->GetValueAsAPSInt();
1281 if (value_or_err)
1282 SetValueFromInteger(*value_or_err, error);
1283 else
1284 error = Status::FromErrorString("error getting APSInt from new_val_sp");
1285 } else if (HasFloatingRepresentation(new_val_type)) {
1286 auto value_or_err = new_val_sp->GetValueAsAPFloat();
1287 if (value_or_err)
1288 SetValueFromInteger(value_or_err->bitcastToAPInt(), error);
1289 else
1290 error = Status::FromErrorString("error getting APFloat from new_val_sp");
1291 } else if (new_val_type.IsPointerType()) {
1292 bool success = true;
1293 uint64_t int_val = new_val_sp->GetValueAsUnsigned(0, &success);
1294 if (success) {
1295 lldb::TargetSP target = GetTargetSP();
1296 uint64_t num_bits = 0;
1297 if (auto temp = llvm::expectedToOptional(
1298 new_val_sp->GetCompilerType().GetBitSize(target.get())))
1299 num_bits = temp.value();
1300 SetValueFromInteger(llvm::APInt(num_bits, int_val), error);
1301 } else
1302 error = Status::FromErrorString("error converting new_val_sp to integer");
1303 }
1304}
1305
1306// if any more "special cases" are added to
1307// ValueObject::DumpPrintableRepresentation() please keep this call up to date
1308// by returning true for your new special cases. We will eventually move to
1309// checking this call result before trying to display special cases
1310bool ValueObject::HasSpecialPrintableRepresentation(
1311 ValueObjectRepresentationStyle val_obj_display, Format custom_format) {
1312 Flags flags(GetTypeInfo());
1313 if (flags.AnySet(eTypeIsArray | eTypeIsPointer) &&
1314 val_obj_display == ValueObject::eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue) {
1315 if (IsCStringContainer(true) &&
1316 (custom_format == eFormatCString || custom_format == eFormatCharArray ||
1317 custom_format == eFormatChar || custom_format == eFormatVectorOfChar))
1318 return true;
1319
1320 if (flags.Test(eTypeIsArray)) {
1321 if ((custom_format == eFormatBytes) ||
1322 (custom_format == eFormatBytesWithASCII))
1323 return true;
1324
1325 if ((custom_format == eFormatVectorOfChar) ||
1326 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfFloat32) ||
1327 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfFloat64) ||
1328 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt16) ||
1329 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt32) ||
1330 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt64) ||
1331 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt8) ||
1332 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt128) ||
1333 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt16) ||
1334 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt32) ||
1335 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt64) ||
1336 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt8))
1337 return true;
1338 }
1339 }
1340 return false;
1341}
1342
1343bool ValueObject::DumpPrintableRepresentation(
1344 Stream &s, ValueObjectRepresentationStyle val_obj_display,
1345 Format custom_format, PrintableRepresentationSpecialCases special,
1346 bool do_dump_error) {
1347
1348 // If the ValueObject has an error, we might end up dumping the type, which
1349 // is useful, but if we don't even have a type, then don't examine the object
1350 // further as that's not meaningful, only the error is.
1351 if (m_error.Fail() && !GetCompilerType().IsValid()) {
1352 if (do_dump_error)
1353 s.Printf("<%s>", m_error.AsCString());
1354 return false;
1355 }
1356
1357 Flags flags(GetTypeInfo());
1358
1359 bool allow_special =
1360 (special == ValueObject::PrintableRepresentationSpecialCases::eAllow);
1361 const bool only_special = false;
1362
1363 if (allow_special) {
1364 if (flags.AnySet(eTypeIsArray | eTypeIsPointer) &&
1365 val_obj_display == ValueObject::eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue) {
1366 // when being asked to get a printable display an array or pointer type
1367 // directly, try to "do the right thing"
1368
1369 if (IsCStringContainer(true) &&
1370 (custom_format == eFormatCString ||
1371 custom_format == eFormatCharArray || custom_format == eFormatChar ||
1372 custom_format ==
1373 eFormatVectorOfChar)) // print char[] & char* directly
1374 {
1375 Status error;
1376 lldb::WritableDataBufferSP buffer_sp;
1377 std::pair<size_t, bool> read_string =
1378 ReadPointedString(buffer_sp, error,
1379 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfChar) ||
1380 (custom_format == eFormatCharArray));
1381 lldb_private::formatters::StringPrinter::
1382 ReadBufferAndDumpToStreamOptions options(*this);
1383 options.SetData(DataExtractor(
1384 buffer_sp, lldb::eByteOrderInvalid,
1385 8)); // none of this matters for a string - pass some defaults
1386 options.SetStream(&s);
1387 options.SetPrefixToken(nullptr);
1388 options.SetQuote('"');
1389 options.SetSourceSize(buffer_sp->GetByteSize());
1390 options.SetIsTruncated(read_string.second);
1391 options.SetBinaryZeroIsTerminator(custom_format != eFormatVectorOfChar);
1392 formatters::StringPrinter::ReadBufferAndDumpToStream<
1393 lldb_private::formatters::StringPrinter::StringElementType::ASCII>(
1394 options);
1395 return !error.Fail();
1396 }
1397
1398 if (custom_format == eFormatEnum)
1399 return false;
1400
1401 // this only works for arrays, because I have no way to know when the
1402 // pointed memory ends, and no special \0 end of data marker
1403 if (flags.Test(eTypeIsArray)) {
1404 if ((custom_format == eFormatBytes) ||
1405 (custom_format == eFormatBytesWithASCII)) {
1406 const size_t count = GetNumChildrenIgnoringErrors();
1407
1408 s << '[';
1409 for (size_t low = 0; low < count; low++) {
1410
1411 if (low)
1412 s << ',';
1413
1414 ValueObjectSP child = GetChildAtIndex(low);
1415 if (!child.get()) {
1416 s << "<invalid child>";
1417 continue;
1418 }
1419 child->DumpPrintableRepresentation(
1420 s, ValueObject::eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue,
1421 custom_format);
1422 }
1423
1424 s << ']';
1425
1426 return true;
1427 }
1428
1429 if ((custom_format == eFormatVectorOfChar) ||
1430 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfFloat32) ||
1431 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfFloat64) ||
1432 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt16) ||
1433 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt32) ||
1434 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt64) ||
1435 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfSInt8) ||
1436 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt128) ||
1437 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt16) ||
1438 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt32) ||
1439 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt64) ||
1440 (custom_format == eFormatVectorOfUInt8)) // arrays of bytes, bytes
1441 // with ASCII or any vector
1442 // format should be printed
1443 // directly
1444 {
1445 const size_t count = GetNumChildrenIgnoringErrors();
1446
1447 Format format = FormatManager::GetSingleItemFormat(custom_format);
1448
1449 s << '[';
1450 for (size_t low = 0; low < count; low++) {
1451
1452 if (low)
1453 s << ',';
1454
1455 ValueObjectSP child = GetChildAtIndex(low);
1456 if (!child.get()) {
1457 s << "<invalid child>";
1458 continue;
1459 }
1460 child->DumpPrintableRepresentation(
1461 s, ValueObject::eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue, format);
1462 }
1463
1464 s << ']';
1465
1466 return true;
1467 }
1468 }
1469
1470 if ((custom_format == eFormatBoolean) ||
1471 (custom_format == eFormatBinary) || (custom_format == eFormatChar) ||
1472 (custom_format == eFormatCharPrintable) ||
1473 (custom_format == eFormatComplexFloat) ||
1474 (custom_format == eFormatDecimal) || (custom_format == eFormatHex) ||
1475 (custom_format == eFormatHexUppercase) ||
1476 (custom_format == eFormatFloat) ||
1477 (custom_format == eFormatFloat128) ||
1478 (custom_format == eFormatOctal) || (custom_format == eFormatOSType) ||
1479 (custom_format == eFormatUnicode16) ||
1480 (custom_format == eFormatUnicode32) ||
1481 (custom_format == eFormatUnsigned) ||
1482 (custom_format == eFormatPointer) ||
1483 (custom_format == eFormatComplexInteger) ||
1484 (custom_format == eFormatComplex) ||
1485 (custom_format == eFormatDefault)) // use the [] operator
1486 return false;
1487 }
1488 }
1489
1490 if (only_special)
1491 return false;
1492
1493 bool var_success = false;
1494
1495 {
1496 llvm::StringRef str;
1497
1498 // this is a local stream that we are using to ensure that the data pointed
1499 // to by cstr survives long enough for us to copy it to its destination -
1500 // it is necessary to have this temporary storage area for cases where our
1501 // desired output is not backed by some other longer-term storage
1502 StreamString strm;
1503
1504 if (custom_format != eFormatInvalid)
1505 SetFormat(custom_format);
1506
1507 switch (val_obj_display) {
1508 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue:
1509 str = GetValueAsCString();
1510 break;
1511
1512 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleSummary:
1513 str = GetSummaryAsCString();
1514 break;
1515
1516 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleLanguageSpecific: {
1517 llvm::Expected<std::string> desc = GetObjectDescription();
1518 if (!desc) {
1519 strm << "error: " << toString(desc.takeError());
1520 str = strm.GetString();
1521 } else {
1522 strm << *desc;
1523 str = strm.GetString();
1524 }
1525 } break;
1526
1527 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleLocation:
1528 str = GetLocationAsCString();
1529 break;
1530
1531 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleChildrenCount: {
1532 if (auto err = GetNumChildren()) {
1533 strm.Printf("%" PRIu32, *err);
1534 str = strm.GetString();
1535 } else {
1536 strm << "error: " << toString(err.takeError());
1537 str = strm.GetString();
1538 }
1539 break;
1540 }
1541
1542 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleType:
1543 str = GetTypeName().GetStringRef();
1544 break;
1545
1546 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleName:
1547 str = GetName().GetStringRef();
1548 break;
1549
1550 case eValueObjectRepresentationStyleExpressionPath:
1551 GetExpressionPath(strm);
1552 str = strm.GetString();
1553 break;
1554 }
1555
1556 // If the requested display style produced no output, try falling back to
1557 // alternative presentations.
1558 if (str.empty()) {
1559 if (val_obj_display == eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue)
1560 str = GetSummaryAsCString();
1561 else if (val_obj_display == eValueObjectRepresentationStyleSummary) {
1562 if (!CanProvideValue()) {
1563 strm.Printf("%s @ %s", GetTypeName().AsCString(),
1564 GetLocationAsCString());
1565 str = strm.GetString();
1566 } else
1567 str = GetValueAsCString();
1568 }
1569 }
1570
1571 if (!str.empty())
1572 s << str;
1573 else {
1574 // We checked for errors at the start, but do it again here in case
1575 // realizing the value for dumping produced an error.
1576 if (m_error.Fail()) {
1577 if (do_dump_error)
1578 s.Printf("<%s>", m_error.AsCString());
1579 else
1580 return false;
1581 } else if (val_obj_display == eValueObjectRepresentationStyleSummary)
1582 s.PutCString("<no summary available>");
1583 else if (val_obj_display == eValueObjectRepresentationStyleValue)
1584 s.PutCString("<no value available>");
1585 else if (val_obj_display ==
1586 eValueObjectRepresentationStyleLanguageSpecific)
1587 s.PutCString("<not a valid Objective-C object>"); // edit this if we
1588 // have other runtimes
1589 // that support a
1590 // description
1591 else
1592 s.PutCString("<no printable representation>");
1593 }
1594
1595 // we should only return false here if we could not do *anything* even if
1596 // we have an error message as output, that's a success from our callers'
1597 // perspective, so return true
1598 var_success = true;
1599
1600 if (custom_format != eFormatInvalid)
1601 SetFormat(eFormatDefault);
1602 }
1603
1604 return var_success;
1605}
1606
1607ValueObject::AddrAndType
1608ValueObject::GetAddressOf(bool scalar_is_load_address) {
1609 // Can't take address of a bitfield
1610 if (IsBitfield())
1611 return {};
1612
1613 if (!UpdateValueIfNeeded(false))
1614 return {};
1615
1616 switch (m_value.GetValueType()) {
1617 case Value::ValueType::Invalid:
1618 return {};
1619 case Value::ValueType::Scalar:
1620 if (scalar_is_load_address) {
1621 return {m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS),
1622 eAddressTypeLoad};
1623 }
1624 return {};
1625
1626 case Value::ValueType::LoadAddress:
1627 case Value::ValueType::FileAddress:
1628 return {m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS),
1629 m_value.GetValueAddressType()};
1630 case Value::ValueType::HostAddress:
1631 return {LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS, m_value.GetValueAddressType()};
1632 }
1633 llvm_unreachable("Unhandled value type!");
1634}
1635
1636ValueObject::AddrAndType ValueObject::GetPointerValue() {
1637 if (!UpdateValueIfNeeded(false))
1638 return {};
1639
1640 switch (m_value.GetValueType()) {
1641 case Value::ValueType::Invalid:
1642 return {};
1643 case Value::ValueType::Scalar:
1644 return {m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS),
1645 GetAddressTypeOfChildren()};
1646
1647 case Value::ValueType::HostAddress:
1648 case Value::ValueType::LoadAddress:
1649 case Value::ValueType::FileAddress: {
1650 lldb::offset_t data_offset = 0;
1651 return {m_data.GetAddress(&data_offset), GetAddressTypeOfChildren()};
1652 }
1653 }
1654
1655 llvm_unreachable("Unhandled value type!");
1656}
1657
1658static const char *ConvertBoolean(lldb::LanguageType language_type,
1659 const char *value_str) {
1660 if (Language *language = Language::FindPlugin(language_type))
1661 if (auto boolean = language->GetBooleanFromString(value_str))
1662 return *boolean ? "1" : "0";
1663
1664 return llvm::StringSwitch<const char *>(value_str)
1665 .Case("true", "1")
1666 .Case("false", "0")
1667 .Default(value_str);
1668}
1669
1670bool ValueObject::SetValueFromCString(const char *value_str, Status &error) {
1671 error.Clear();
1672 // Make sure our value is up to date first so that our location and location
1673 // type is valid.
1674 if (!UpdateValueIfNeeded(false)) {
1675 error = Status::FromErrorString("unable to read value");
1676 return false;
1677 }
1678
1679 const Encoding encoding = GetCompilerType().GetEncoding();
1680
1681 const size_t byte_size = llvm::expectedToOptional(GetByteSize()).value_or(0);
1682
1683 Value::ValueType value_type = m_value.GetValueType();
1684
1685 if (value_type == Value::ValueType::Scalar) {
1686 // If the value is already a scalar, then let the scalar change itself:
1687 m_value.GetScalar().SetValueFromCString(value_str, encoding, byte_size);
1688 } else if (byte_size <= 16) {
1689 if (GetCompilerType().IsBoolean())
1690 value_str = ConvertBoolean(GetObjectRuntimeLanguage(), value_str);
1691
1692 // If the value fits in a scalar, then make a new scalar and again let the
1693 // scalar code do the conversion, then figure out where to put the new
1694 // value.
1695 Scalar new_scalar;
1696 error = new_scalar.SetValueFromCString(value_str, encoding, byte_size);
1697 if (error.Success()) {
1698 switch (value_type) {
1699 case Value::ValueType::LoadAddress: {
1700 // If it is a load address, then the scalar value is the storage
1701 // location of the data, and we have to shove this value down to that
1702 // load location.
1703 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
1704 Process *process = exe_ctx.GetProcessPtr();
1705 if (process) {
1706 addr_t target_addr =
1707 m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
1708 size_t bytes_written = process->WriteScalarToMemory(
1709 target_addr, new_scalar, byte_size, error);
1710 if (!error.Success())
1711 return false;
1712 if (bytes_written != byte_size) {
1713 error = Status::FromErrorString("unable to write value to memory");
1714 return false;
1715 }
1716 }
1717 } break;
1718 case Value::ValueType::HostAddress: {
1719 // If it is a host address, then we stuff the scalar as a DataBuffer
1720 // into the Value's data.
1721 DataExtractor new_data;
1722 new_data.SetByteOrder(m_data.GetByteOrder());
1723
1724 DataBufferSP buffer_sp(new DataBufferHeap(byte_size, 0));
1725 m_data.SetData(buffer_sp, 0);
1726 bool success = new_scalar.GetData(new_data);
1727 if (success) {
1728 new_data.CopyByteOrderedData(
1729 0, byte_size, const_cast<uint8_t *>(m_data.GetDataStart()),
1730 byte_size, m_data.GetByteOrder());
1731 }
1732 m_value.GetScalar() = (uintptr_t)m_data.GetDataStart();
1733
1734 } break;
1735 case Value::ValueType::Invalid:
1736 error = Status::FromErrorString("invalid location");
1737 return false;
1738 case Value::ValueType::FileAddress:
1739 case Value::ValueType::Scalar:
1740 break;
1741 }
1742 } else {
1743 return false;
1744 }
1745 } else {
1746 // We don't support setting things bigger than a scalar at present.
1747 error = Status::FromErrorString("unable to write aggregate data type");
1748 return false;
1749 }
1750
1751 // If we have reached this point, then we have successfully changed the
1752 // value.
1753 SetNeedsUpdate();
1754 return true;
1755}
1756
1757bool ValueObject::GetDeclaration(Declaration &decl) {
1758 decl.Clear();
1759 return false;
1760}
1761
1762void ValueObject::AddSyntheticChild(ConstString key, ValueObject *valobj) {
1763 m_synthetic_children[key] = valobj;
1764}
1765
1766ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticChild(ConstString key) const {
1767 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1768 std::map<ConstString, ValueObject *>::const_iterator pos =
1769 m_synthetic_children.find(key);
1770 if (pos != m_synthetic_children.end())
1771 synthetic_child_sp = pos->second->GetSP();
1772 return synthetic_child_sp;
1773}
1774
1775bool ValueObject::IsPossibleDynamicType() {
1776 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
1777 Process *process = exe_ctx.GetProcessPtr();
1778 if (process)
1779 return process->IsPossibleDynamicValue(*this);
1780 else
1781 return GetCompilerType().IsPossibleDynamicType(nullptr, true, true);
1782}
1783
1784bool ValueObject::IsRuntimeSupportValue() {
1785 Process *process(GetProcessSP().get());
1786 if (!process)
1787 return false;
1788
1789 // We trust that the compiler did the right thing and marked runtime support
1790 // values as artificial.
1791 if (!GetVariable() || !GetVariable()->IsArtificial())
1792 return false;
1793
1794 if (auto *runtime = process->GetLanguageRuntime(GetVariable()->GetLanguage()))
1795 if (runtime->IsAllowedRuntimeValue(GetName()))
1796 return false;
1797
1798 return true;
1799}
1800
1801bool ValueObject::IsNilReference() {
1802 if (Language *language = Language::FindPlugin(GetObjectRuntimeLanguage())) {
1803 return language->IsNilReference(*this);
1804 }
1805 return false;
1806}
1807
1808bool ValueObject::IsUninitializedReference() {
1809 if (Language *language = Language::FindPlugin(GetObjectRuntimeLanguage())) {
1810 return language->IsUninitializedReference(*this);
1811 }
1812 return false;
1813}
1814
1815// This allows you to create an array member using and index that doesn't not
1816// fall in the normal bounds of the array. Many times structure can be defined
1817// as: struct Collection {
1818// uint32_t item_count;
1819// Item item_array[0];
1820// };
1821// The size of the "item_array" is 1, but many times in practice there are more
1822// items in "item_array".
1823
1824ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticArrayMember(size_t index,
1825 bool can_create) {
1826 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1827 if (IsPointerType() || IsArrayType()) {
1828 std::string index_str = llvm::formatv("[{0}]", index);
1829 ConstString index_const_str(index_str);
1830 // Check if we have already created a synthetic array member in this valid
1831 // object. If we have we will re-use it.
1832 synthetic_child_sp = GetSyntheticChild(index_const_str);
1833 if (!synthetic_child_sp) {
1834 ValueObject *synthetic_child;
1835 // We haven't made a synthetic array member for INDEX yet, so lets make
1836 // one and cache it for any future reference.
1837 synthetic_child = CreateSyntheticArrayMember(index);
1838
1839 // Cache the value if we got one back...
1840 if (synthetic_child) {
1841 AddSyntheticChild(index_const_str, synthetic_child);
1842 synthetic_child_sp = synthetic_child->GetSP();
1843 synthetic_child_sp->SetName(ConstString(index_str));
1844 synthetic_child_sp->m_flags.m_is_array_item_for_pointer = true;
1845 }
1846 }
1847 }
1848 return synthetic_child_sp;
1849}
1850
1851ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticBitFieldChild(uint32_t from, uint32_t to,
1852 bool can_create) {
1853 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1854 if (IsScalarType()) {
1855 std::string index_str = llvm::formatv("[{0}-{1}]", from, to);
1856 ConstString index_const_str(index_str);
1857 // Check if we have already created a synthetic array member in this valid
1858 // object. If we have we will re-use it.
1859 synthetic_child_sp = GetSyntheticChild(index_const_str);
1860 if (!synthetic_child_sp) {
1861 uint32_t bit_field_size = to - from + 1;
1862 uint32_t bit_field_offset = from;
1863 if (GetDataExtractor().GetByteOrder() == eByteOrderBig)
1864 bit_field_offset =
1865 llvm::expectedToOptional(GetByteSize()).value_or(0) * 8 -
1866 bit_field_size - bit_field_offset;
1867 // We haven't made a synthetic array member for INDEX yet, so lets make
1868 // one and cache it for any future reference.
1869 ValueObjectChild *synthetic_child = new ValueObjectChild(
1870 *this, GetCompilerType(), index_const_str,
1871 llvm::expectedToOptional(GetByteSize()).value_or(0), 0,
1872 bit_field_size, bit_field_offset, false, false, eAddressTypeInvalid,
1873 0);
1874
1875 // Cache the value if we got one back...
1876 if (synthetic_child) {
1877 AddSyntheticChild(index_const_str, synthetic_child);
1878 synthetic_child_sp = synthetic_child->GetSP();
1879 synthetic_child_sp->SetName(ConstString(index_str));
1880 synthetic_child_sp->m_flags.m_is_bitfield_for_scalar = true;
1881 }
1882 }
1883 }
1884 return synthetic_child_sp;
1885}
1886
1887ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticChildAtOffset(
1888 uint32_t offset, const CompilerType &type, bool can_create,
1889 ConstString name_const_str) {
1890
1891 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1892
1893 if (name_const_str.IsEmpty()) {
1894 name_const_str.SetString("@" + std::to_string(offset));
1895 }
1896
1897 // Check if we have already created a synthetic array member in this valid
1898 // object. If we have we will re-use it.
1899 synthetic_child_sp = GetSyntheticChild(name_const_str);
1900
1901 if (synthetic_child_sp.get())
1902 return synthetic_child_sp;
1903
1904 if (!can_create)
1905 return {};
1906
1907 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
1908 std::optional<uint64_t> size = llvm::expectedToOptional(
1909 type.GetByteSize(exe_ctx.GetBestExecutionContextScope()));
1910 if (!size)
1911 return {};
1912 ValueObjectChild *synthetic_child =
1913 new ValueObjectChild(*this, type, name_const_str, *size, offset, 0, 0,
1914 false, false, eAddressTypeInvalid, 0);
1915 if (synthetic_child) {
1916 AddSyntheticChild(name_const_str, synthetic_child);
1917 synthetic_child_sp = synthetic_child->GetSP();
1918 synthetic_child_sp->SetName(name_const_str);
1919 synthetic_child_sp->m_flags.m_is_child_at_offset = true;
1920 }
1921 return synthetic_child_sp;
1922}
1923
1924ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticBase(uint32_t offset,
1925 const CompilerType &type,
1926 bool can_create,
1927 ConstString name_const_str) {
1928 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1929
1930 if (name_const_str.IsEmpty()) {
1931 char name_str[128];
1932 snprintf(name_str, sizeof(name_str), "base%s@%i",
1933 type.GetTypeName().AsCString("<unknown>"), offset);
1934 name_const_str.SetCString(name_str);
1935 }
1936
1937 // Check if we have already created a synthetic array member in this valid
1938 // object. If we have we will re-use it.
1939 synthetic_child_sp = GetSyntheticChild(name_const_str);
1940
1941 if (synthetic_child_sp.get())
1942 return synthetic_child_sp;
1943
1944 if (!can_create)
1945 return {};
1946
1947 const bool is_base_class = true;
1948
1949 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
1950 std::optional<uint64_t> size = llvm::expectedToOptional(
1951 type.GetByteSize(exe_ctx.GetBestExecutionContextScope()));
1952 if (!size)
1953 return {};
1954 ValueObjectChild *synthetic_child =
1955 new ValueObjectChild(*this, type, name_const_str, *size, offset, 0, 0,
1956 is_base_class, false, eAddressTypeInvalid, 0);
1957 if (synthetic_child) {
1958 AddSyntheticChild(name_const_str, synthetic_child);
1959 synthetic_child_sp = synthetic_child->GetSP();
1960 synthetic_child_sp->SetName(name_const_str);
1961 }
1962 return synthetic_child_sp;
1963}
1964
1965// your expression path needs to have a leading . or -> (unless it somehow
1966// "looks like" an array, in which case it has a leading [ symbol). while the [
1967// is meaningful and should be shown to the user, . and -> are just parser
1968// design, but by no means added information for the user.. strip them off
1969static const char *SkipLeadingExpressionPathSeparators(const char *expression) {
1970 if (!expression || !expression[0])
1971 return expression;
1972 if (expression[0] == '.')
1973 return expression + 1;
1974 if (expression[0] == '-' && expression[1] == '>')
1975 return expression + 2;
1976 return expression;
1977}
1978
1979ValueObjectSP
1980ValueObject::GetSyntheticExpressionPathChild(const char *expression,
1981 bool can_create) {
1982 ValueObjectSP synthetic_child_sp;
1983 ConstString name_const_string(expression);
1984 // Check if we have already created a synthetic array member in this valid
1985 // object. If we have we will re-use it.
1986 synthetic_child_sp = GetSyntheticChild(name_const_string);
1987 if (!synthetic_child_sp) {
1988 // We haven't made a synthetic array member for expression yet, so lets
1989 // make one and cache it for any future reference.
1990 synthetic_child_sp = GetValueForExpressionPath(
1991 expression, nullptr, nullptr,
1992 GetValueForExpressionPathOptions().SetSyntheticChildrenTraversal(
1993 GetValueForExpressionPathOptions::SyntheticChildrenTraversal::
1994 None));
1995
1996 // Cache the value if we got one back...
1997 if (synthetic_child_sp.get()) {
1998 // FIXME: this causes a "real" child to end up with its name changed to
1999 // the contents of expression
2000 AddSyntheticChild(name_const_string, synthetic_child_sp.get());
2001 synthetic_child_sp->SetName(
2002 ConstString(SkipLeadingExpressionPathSeparators(expression)));
2003 }
2004 }
2005 return synthetic_child_sp;
2006}
2007
2008void ValueObject::CalculateSyntheticValue() {
2009 TargetSP target_sp(GetTargetSP());
2010 if (target_sp && !target_sp->GetEnableSyntheticValue()) {
2011 m_synthetic_value = nullptr;
2012 return;
2013 }
2014
2015 lldb::SyntheticChildrenSP current_synth_sp(m_synthetic_children_sp);
2016
2017 if (!UpdateFormatsIfNeeded() && m_synthetic_value)
2018 return;
2019
2020 if (m_synthetic_children_sp.get() == nullptr)
2021 return;
2022
2023 if (current_synth_sp == m_synthetic_children_sp && m_synthetic_value)
2024 return;
2025
2026 m_synthetic_value = new ValueObjectSynthetic(*this, m_synthetic_children_sp);
2027}
2028
2029void ValueObject::CalculateDynamicValue(DynamicValueType use_dynamic) {
2030 if (use_dynamic == eNoDynamicValues)
2031 return;
2032
2033 if (!m_dynamic_value && !IsDynamic()) {
2034 ExecutionContext exe_ctx(GetExecutionContextRef());
2035 Process *process = exe_ctx.GetProcessPtr();
2036 if (process && process->IsPossibleDynamicValue(*this)) {
2037 ClearDynamicTypeInformation();
2038 m_dynamic_value = new ValueObjectDynamicValue(*this, use_dynamic);
2039 }
2040 }
2041}
2042
2043ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetDynamicValue(DynamicValueType use_dynamic) {
2044 if (use_dynamic == eNoDynamicValues)
2045 return ValueObjectSP();
2046
2047 if (!IsDynamic() && m_dynamic_value == nullptr) {
2048 CalculateDynamicValue(use_dynamic);
2049 }
2050 if (m_dynamic_value && m_dynamic_value->GetError().Success())
2051 return m_dynamic_value->GetSP();
2052 else
2053 return ValueObjectSP();
2054}
2055
2056ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetSyntheticValue() {
2057 CalculateSyntheticValue();
2058
2059 if (m_synthetic_value)
2060 return m_synthetic_value->GetSP();
2061 else
2062 return ValueObjectSP();
2063}
2064
2065bool ValueObject::HasSyntheticValue() {
2066 UpdateFormatsIfNeeded();
2067
2068 if (m_synthetic_children_sp.get() == nullptr)
2069 return false;
2070
2071 CalculateSyntheticValue();
2072
2073 return m_synthetic_value != nullptr;
2074}
2075
2076ValueObject *ValueObject::GetNonBaseClassParent() {
2077 if (GetParent()) {
2078 if (GetParent()->IsBaseClass())
2079 return GetParent()->GetNonBaseClassParent();
2080 else
2081 return GetParent();
2082 }
2083 return nullptr;
2084}
2085
2086bool ValueObject::IsBaseClass(uint32_t &depth) {
2087 if (!IsBaseClass()) {
2088 depth = 0;
2089 return false;
2090 }
2091 if (GetParent()) {
2092 GetParent()->IsBaseClass(depth);
2093 depth = depth + 1;
2094 return true;
2095 }
2096 // TODO: a base of no parent? weird..
2097 depth = 1;
2098 return true;
2099}
2100
2101void ValueObject::GetExpressionPath(Stream &s,
2102 GetExpressionPathFormat epformat) {
2103 // synthetic children do not actually "exist" as part of the hierarchy, and
2104 // sometimes they are consed up in ways that don't make sense from an
2105 // underlying language/API standpoint. So, use a special code path here to
2106 // return something that can hopefully be used in expression
2107 if (m_flags.m_is_synthetic_children_generated) {
2108 UpdateValueIfNeeded();
2109
2110 if (m_value.GetValueType() == Value::ValueType::LoadAddress) {
2111 if (IsPointerOrReferenceType()) {
2112 s.Printf("((%s)0x%" PRIx64 ")", GetTypeName().AsCString("void"),
2113 GetValueAsUnsigned(0));
2114 return;
2115 } else {
2116 uint64_t load_addr =
2117 m_value.GetScalar().ULongLong(LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS);
2118 if (load_addr != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS) {
2119 s.Printf("(*( (%s *)0x%" PRIx64 "))", GetTypeName().AsCString("void"),
2120 load_addr);
2121 return;
2122 }
2123 }
2124 }
2125
2126 if (CanProvideValue()) {
2127 s.Printf("((%s)%s)", GetTypeName().AsCString("void"),
2128 GetValueAsCString());
2129 return;
2130 }
2131
2132 return;
2133 }
2134
2135 const bool is_deref_of_parent = IsDereferenceOfParent();
2136
2137 if (is_deref_of_parent &&
2138 epformat == eGetExpressionPathFormatDereferencePointers) {
2139 // this is the original format of GetExpressionPath() producing code like
2140 // *(a_ptr).memberName, which is entirely fine, until you put this into
2141 // StackFrame::GetValueForVariableExpressionPath() which prefers to see
2142 // a_ptr->memberName. the eHonorPointers mode is meant to produce strings
2143 // in this latter format
2144 s.PutCString("*(");
2145 }
2146
2147 ValueObject *parent = GetParent();
2148
2149 if (parent) {
2150 parent->GetExpressionPath(s, epformat);
2151 const CompilerType parentType = parent->GetCompilerType();
2152 if (parentType.IsPointerType() &&
2153 parentType.GetPointeeType().IsArrayType(nullptr, nullptr, nullptr)) {
2154 // When the parent is a pointer to an array, then we have to:
2155 // - follow the expression path of the parent with "[0]"
2156 // (that will indicate dereferencing the pointer to the array)
2157 // - and then follow that with this ValueObject's name
2158 // (which will be something like "[i]" to indicate
2159 // the i-th element of the array)
2160 s.PutCString("[0]");
2161 s.PutCString(GetName().GetCString());
2162 return;
2163 }
2164 }
2165
2166 // if we are a deref_of_parent just because we are synthetic array members
2167 // made up to allow ptr[%d] syntax to work in variable printing, then add our
2168 // name ([%d]) to the expression path
2169 if (m_flags.m_is_array_item_for_pointer &&
2170 epformat == eGetExpressionPathFormatHonorPointers)
2171 s.PutCString(m_name.GetStringRef());
2172
2173 if (!IsBaseClass()) {
2174 if (!is_deref_of_parent) {
2175 ValueObject *non_base_class_parent = GetNonBaseClassParent();
2176 if (non_base_class_parent &&
2177 !non_base_class_parent->GetName().IsEmpty()) {
2178 CompilerType non_base_class_parent_compiler_type =
2179 non_base_class_parent->GetCompilerType();
2180 if (non_base_class_parent_compiler_type) {
2181 if (parent && parent->IsDereferenceOfParent() &&
2182 epformat == eGetExpressionPathFormatHonorPointers) {
2183 s.PutCString("->");
2184 } else {
2185 const uint32_t non_base_class_parent_type_info =
2186 non_base_class_parent_compiler_type.GetTypeInfo();
2187
2188 if (non_base_class_parent_type_info & eTypeIsPointer) {
2189 s.PutCString("->");
2190 } else if ((non_base_class_parent_type_info & eTypeHasChildren) &&
2191 !(non_base_class_parent_type_info & eTypeIsArray)) {
2192 s.PutChar('.');
2193 }
2194 }
2195 }
2196 }
2197
2198 const char *name = GetName().GetCString();
2199 if (name)
2200 s.PutCString(name);
2201 }
2202 }
2203
2204 if (is_deref_of_parent &&
2205 epformat == eGetExpressionPathFormatDereferencePointers) {
2206 s.PutChar(')');
2207 }
2208}
2209
2210// Return the alternate value (synthetic if the input object is non-synthetic
2211// and otherwise) this is permitted by the expression path options.
2212static ValueObjectSP GetAlternateValue(
2213 ValueObject &valobj,
2214 ValueObject::GetValueForExpressionPathOptions::SyntheticChildrenTraversal
2215 synth_traversal) {
2216 using SynthTraversal =
2217 ValueObject::GetValueForExpressionPathOptions::SyntheticChildrenTraversal;
2218
2219 if (valobj.IsSynthetic()) {
2220 if (synth_traversal == SynthTraversal::FromSynthetic ||
2221 synth_traversal == SynthTraversal::Both)
2222 return valobj.GetNonSyntheticValue();
2223 } else {
2224 if (synth_traversal == SynthTraversal::ToSynthetic ||
2225 synth_traversal == SynthTraversal::Both)
2226 return valobj.GetSyntheticValue();
2227 }
2228 return nullptr;
2229}
2230
2231// Dereference the provided object or the alternate value, if permitted by the
2232// expression path options.
2233static ValueObjectSP DereferenceValueOrAlternate(
2234 ValueObject &valobj,
2235 ValueObject::GetValueForExpressionPathOptions::SyntheticChildrenTraversal
2236 synth_traversal,
2237 Status &error) {
2238 error.Clear();
2239 ValueObjectSP result = valobj.Dereference(error);
2240 if (!result || error.Fail()) {
2241 if (ValueObjectSP alt_obj = GetAlternateValue(valobj, synth_traversal)) {
2242 error.Clear();
2243 result = alt_obj->Dereference(error);
2244 }
2245 }
2246 return result;
2247}
2248
2249ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetValueForExpressionPath(
2250 llvm::StringRef expression, ExpressionPathScanEndReason *reason_to_stop,
2251 ExpressionPathEndResultType *final_value_type,
2252 const GetValueForExpressionPathOptions &options,
2253 ExpressionPathAftermath *final_task_on_target) {
2254
2255 ExpressionPathScanEndReason dummy_reason_to_stop =
2256 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonUnknown;
2257 ExpressionPathEndResultType dummy_final_value_type =
2258 ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2259 ExpressionPathAftermath dummy_final_task_on_target =
2260 ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathNothing;
2261
2262 ValueObjectSP ret_val = GetValueForExpressionPath_Impl(
2263 expression, reason_to_stop ? reason_to_stop : &dummy_reason_to_stop,
2264 final_value_type ? final_value_type : &dummy_final_value_type, options,
2265 final_task_on_target ? final_task_on_target
2266 : &dummy_final_task_on_target);
2267
2268 if (!final_task_on_target ||
2269 *final_task_on_target == ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathNothing)
2270 return ret_val;
2271
2272 if (ret_val.get() &&
2273 ((final_value_type ? *final_value_type : dummy_final_value_type) ==
2274 eExpressionPathEndResultTypePlain)) // I can only deref and takeaddress
2275 // of plain objects
2276 {
2277 if ((final_task_on_target ? *final_task_on_target
2278 : dummy_final_task_on_target) ==
2279 ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathDereference) {
2280 Status error;
2281 ValueObjectSP final_value = DereferenceValueOrAlternate(
2282 *ret_val, options.m_synthetic_children_traversal, error);
2283 if (error.Fail() || !final_value.get()) {
2284 if (reason_to_stop)
2285 *reason_to_stop =
2286 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonDereferencingFailed;
2287 if (final_value_type)
2288 *final_value_type = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2289 return ValueObjectSP();
2290 } else {
2291 if (final_task_on_target)
2292 *final_task_on_target = ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathNothing;
2293 return final_value;
2294 }
2295 }
2296 if (*final_task_on_target ==
2297 ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathTakeAddress) {
2298 Status error;
2299 ValueObjectSP final_value = ret_val->AddressOf(error);
2300 if (error.Fail() || !final_value.get()) {
2301 if (reason_to_stop)
2302 *reason_to_stop =
2303 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonTakingAddressFailed;
2304 if (final_value_type)
2305 *final_value_type = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2306 return ValueObjectSP();
2307 } else {
2308 if (final_task_on_target)
2309 *final_task_on_target = ValueObject::eExpressionPathAftermathNothing;
2310 return final_value;
2311 }
2312 }
2313 }
2314 return ret_val; // final_task_on_target will still have its original value, so
2315 // you know I did not do it
2316}
2317
2318ValueObjectSP ValueObject::GetValueForExpressionPath_Impl(
2319 llvm::StringRef expression, ExpressionPathScanEndReason *reason_to_stop,
2320 ExpressionPathEndResultType *final_result,
2321 const GetValueForExpressionPathOptions &options,
2322 ExpressionPathAftermath *what_next) {
2323 ValueObjectSP root = GetSP();
2324
2325 if (!root)
2326 return nullptr;
2327
2328 llvm::StringRef remainder = expression;
2329
2330 while (true) {
2331 llvm::StringRef temp_expression = remainder;
2332
2333 CompilerType root_compiler_type = root->GetCompilerType();
2334 CompilerType pointee_compiler_type;
2335 Flags pointee_compiler_type_info;
2336
2337 Flags root_compiler_type_info(
2338 root_compiler_type.GetTypeInfo(&pointee_compiler_type));
2339 if (pointee_compiler_type)
2340 pointee_compiler_type_info.Reset(pointee_compiler_type.GetTypeInfo());
2341
2342 if (temp_expression.empty()) {
2343 *reason_to_stop = ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonEndOfString;
2344 return root;
2345 }
2346
2347 switch (temp_expression.front()) {
2348 case '-': {
2349 temp_expression = temp_expression.drop_front();
2350 if (options.m_check_dot_vs_arrow_syntax &&
2351 root_compiler_type_info.Test(eTypeIsPointer)) // if you are trying to
2352 // use -> on a
2353 // non-pointer and I
2354 // must catch the error
2355 {
2356 *reason_to_stop =
2357 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonArrowInsteadOfDot;
2358 *final_result = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2359 return ValueObjectSP();
2360 }
2361 if (root_compiler_type_info.Test(eTypeIsObjC) && // if yo are trying to
2362 // extract an ObjC IVar
2363 // when this is forbidden
2364 root_compiler_type_info.Test(eTypeIsPointer) &&
2365 options.m_no_fragile_ivar) {
2366 *reason_to_stop =
2367 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonFragileIVarNotAllowed;
2368 *final_result = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2369 return ValueObjectSP();
2370 }
2371 if (!temp_expression.starts_with(">")) {
2372 *reason_to_stop =
2373 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonUnexpectedSymbol;
2374 *final_result = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
2375 return ValueObjectSP();
2376 }
2377 }
2378 [[fallthrough]];
2379 case '.': // or fallthrough from ->
2380 {
2381 if (options.m_check_dot_vs_arrow_syntax &&
2382 temp_expression.front() == '.' &&
2383 root_compiler_type_info.Test(eTypeIsPointer)) // if you are trying to
2384 // use . on a pointer
2385 // and I must catch the
2386 // error
2387 {
2388 *reason_to_stop =
2389 ValueObject::eExpressionPathScanEndReasonDotInsteadOfArrow;
2390 *final_result = ValueObject::eExpressionPathEndResultTypeInvalid;
